vitals.terrain: Mixed terrain with gravel routes on June 6 and road cyclosport routes on June 7
vitals.aid_stations: Multiple refreshment stations with energy supplies
vitals.location: Vaison-la-Romaine, Vaucluse, France (Mont Ventoux summit finish)

course_description.overview: The Colnago Gran Fondo Mont Ventoux is a legendary French cyclosportive featuring a summit finish on Mont Ventoux, the 'Giant of Provence.' The event takes place over two days with gravel exploration routes on Saturday and three road cyclosport routes on Sunday, all culminating at nearly 2000 m altitude on Mont Ventoux. The course traverses winding roads through Provençal villages, lavender fields, and the Gorges de la Nesque.

key_segments.name: Mont Ventoux
key_segments.description: The iconic final ascent to approximately 1910 m, the defining feature of the event with a summit finish

key_segments.name: Gorges de la Nesque
key_segments.description: Scenic gorge section featuring stunning landscape and winding roads

key_segments.name: Col de Fontaube
key_segments.description: Reconnaissance climb used in pre-event warm-up rides

course_description.difficulty_narrative: The Granfondo (135 km, 3600 m elevation) represents a significant challenge with sustained climbing and high elevation gain. The event is designed to test riders' endurance and climbing ability, culminating in the demanding Mont Ventoux summit finish. Mediofondo and Piccolofondo options provide accessible alternatives with reduced distance and elevation.

logistics.getting_there: Located in Vaison-la-Romaine, Provence. Marseille Airport serves as the primary access point with organized transfers available through tour operators.
logistics.accommodation: Vaison-la-Romaine town square hosts the athletes village and event expo. Multiple accommodation options available in the region with tour operators offering package deals (3-4 night packages starting from £699 GBP).
logistics.registration_details: Registration opened November 1, 2025. Saturday registration at the athletes village in Vaison-la-Romaine town square. Participants receive free jerseys and goodie bags. Minimum age: 18 for Granfondo, 17 for Mediofondo (with parental authorization), 15 for Piccolofondo. Event begins at 07:30 AM on Sunday June 7.
logistics.bike_logistics: Shuttle service available from Mont Serein to Mont Ventoux summit (€2.50, free for under-12s). Number collection card with QR code provided via email Thursday before race.
logistics.local_considerations: Event expo open 10:00 AM to 7:00 PM on Saturday. Warm-up rides available Friday. Post-race awards ceremonies scheduled for 3:30 PM. Expected first Granfondo finishers at summit around 12:00 PM, with last riders arriving by 3:30 PM.

final_verdict.one_liner: A notable French cyclosportive with iconic Mont Ventoux summit finish, excellent organization, and stunning Provençal scenery that rivals top European sportives.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced cyclists seeking a prestigious European gran fondo with significant climbing challenge. Suitable for riders with strong endurance and climbing fitness. Multiple distance options (80-135 km) accommodate varying ability levels.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes. The event combines legendary status, professional organization, scenic beauty, and the iconic Mont Ventoux challenge. Registration fees are reasonable (€91-116 depending on timing), though travel and accommodation costs are significant. The experience justifies the investment for serious cyclists.
pro_tips: Register early to secure preferred pricing (€91 for first 500 riders)
Arrive Friday for warm-up reconnaissance rides over Col de Fontaube to acclimate
Plan accommodation in Vaison-la-Romaine for convenient access to athletes village and start line
Consider gravel routes on Saturday June 6 for additional exploration and acclimatization before Sunday's main event
